:::{#thm-deriv-invodds}

#### Derivative of inverse odds function
$$\dinvoddsf{\odds} = \derivf{\prob}{\odds} = (1-\prob)^2 = \frac{1}{\sqf{1+\odds}} $${#eq-d_prob-d_odds}
:::

{{< slidebreak >}}

::: proof

Using the [inverse function rule](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Inverse_function_rule),
@thm-deriv-odds,
and @lem-one-minus-odds-inv:

$$
\ba
\derivf{\prob}{\odds}
&= \invf{\derivf{\odds}{\prob}}
&& \text{(inverse function rule)}
\\ &= \invf{\frac{1}{\sqf{1-\prob}}}
&& \text{(derivative of odds function)}
\\ &= \sqf{1-\prob}
&& \text{(the reciprocal of a reciprocal is the original quantity)}
\\ &= \sqf{\frac{1}{1+\odds}}
&& \text{(substitute } 1-\prob = \frac{1}{1+\odds}\text{)}
\\ &= \frac{1}{\sqf{1+\odds}}
&& \text{(the square of a reciprocal is the reciprocal of the square)}
\ea
$$

:::: notes
Or for a direct approach,
use the [quotient rule](math-prereqs.qmd#thm-quotient-rule) again:
::::

$$
\ba
\derivf{\prob}{\odds}
&= \deriv{\odds} \frac{\odds}{1+\odds}
\\ &= \frac{\deriv{\odds}\odds}{1+\odds} - \frac{\odds}{\sqf{1+\odds}} \cd \deriv{\odds}(1+\odds)
\\ &= \frac{1}{1+\odds} - \frac{\odds}{\sqf{1+\odds}} \cd 1
\\ &= \frac{1}{1+\odds} - \frac{\odds}{\sqf{1+\odds}}
\\ &= \frac{1+\odds}{\sqf{1+\odds}} - \frac{\odds}{\sqf{1+\odds}}
\\ &= \frac{1+\odds - \odds}{\sqf{1+\odds}}
\\ &= \frac{1}{\sqf{1+\odds}}
\ea
$$

:::
